rulings.Case Title: MILES HENRY COBB SELLAI VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion For Court To Determine Amount Of Medi-Cal Lien
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Friday, February 21, 2025, Line 1. OTHER MICHELLE BAASS DIRECTOR OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH CARE SERVICES Amended Motion For Court To Determine Amount Of Medi-Cal Lien. This matter is returned to Department 505 for adjudication on Wed. 4/9/25 at 9:00am. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: NOB HILL CONSTRUCTION, INC., VS. HARLAN L. SOUTH ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Friday, February 21, 2025, Line 7. 1 - CROSS DEFENDANTs NOB HILL CONSTRUCTION, INC., and AMERICAN CONTRACTORS INDEMNITY COMPANY, DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained to the entire Cross Complaint based on Cross-Complainant's failure to comply with California Rules of Court, Rule 2.112(4). This defect is so obvious and indisputable the court has trouble understanding how, if the parties were taking their meet and confer obligations seriously, they could not resolve the issue without court intervention. Should there be subsequent pleadings or motions requiring meet and confer, the court will closely review the meet and confer declaration to ensure compliance. The court need not-and does not-adjudicate Cross-defendants' other contentions at this time. Cross-Complainants are granted leave to amend. In their First Amended Cross Complaint, Cross-Complainants shall explicitly indicate for each cause of action, the party or parties from whom relief is sought. Cross-Complainants shall have 20 days from notice of this order to file a first amended cross-complaint. Cross-Defendants to submit a proposed order tracking the substance of the tentative verbatim. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/JMQ)

Cross-Defendants Nob Hill Construction, Inc. and American Contractors Indemnity Company's motion to strike Cross-complainants Harlan L. rulings.Case Title: CROWN ASSET MANAGEMENT, LLC VS. ALEJANDRO AVILA
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Friday, February 21, 2025, Line 9. DEFENDANT ALEJANDRO AVILA's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Specially Appearing Defendant Alejandro Avila's unopposed motion to quash service of summons, stay or dismiss action is GRANTED. The record demonstrates Plaintiff Crown Asset Management, LLC has not effectuated substitute service on the Specially Appearing Defendant and, thus, the court is currently without jurisdiction over him for purposes of this action. (See CCP section 418.10(a)(1).) The proof of service filed on December 26, 2025, is quashed and of no effect. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/JMQ)
